{"key_event":"National Front consolidation and developmental-state expansion","reasoning":"The National Front reduced elite conflict and expanded planning, agrarian, educational, and coercive capacities, but persistent rural enclaves, emerging guerrillas, and legalized civilian self-defense kept state reach below fully strong levels. Civil society recovered and diversified through MRL opposition, students, labor, and peasant organization, yet bipartisan exclusion and counterinsurgency constrained autonomous accountability.","society_change":0.4,"society_power":5.8,"state_change":0.3,"state_power":6.9}
